Title: Free (feat. Emeli Sandé)
Comment:
When the beach is calling and the horizon stretches wide, Rudimental – Free slips effortlessly into the scene. This track isn't just a song—it’s an anthem of liberation, with Emeli Sandé’s vocals soaring like a salty breeze across sunlit waters. Its uplifting melody and steady beat blend perfectly with the rhythm of waves, making it ideal for those golden hours spent sprawled on the sand or gliding down the coastline with the windows down.

The chorus, “Try to find my way back, find my way back”, echoes like a personal mantra for anyone taking a mental escape from everyday stress. It’s the kind of tune that invites you to close your eyes and let your thoughts drift, while the energy stays buoyant enough to keep spirits high.

For a beachside listening mix, Free is more than a vibe, it’s a mood lifter wrapped in sunshine.

Title: Send the Pain Below
Comment: Let me paint you a picture. My parents, in all their infinite wisdom, think it's a great idea to take their 12-year-old daughter to a festival to see her favorite band for her 13th birthday (Chevelle was not that band). Anyway, they took me to Rock on the Range in 2007 in Columbus, OH. I saw my favorite band (Evanescence), but Chevelle's set is the one that shaped me for the rest of my life. My parents and I still remember this. My dad and I lost my mom in the put during their set.
